channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Netanyahu says Iran 'will pay' for missile attack
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Iran “will pay” for the 180 missiles launched toward his country on Tuesday. “This evening, Iran made a big mistake — and it will pay for it,” the leader said during a security Cabinet meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A plane carrying Brazil's president back home is returning to Mexico City due to a technical problem
item.description: Brazil’s air force says that the airplane carrying Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva experienced a technical problem after leaving Mexico City and will return to the country’s capital so he can board another flight to return home.
item.dc:creator: Brazil Lula Plane Mexico City Inauguration, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Iran's missile barrage and Israel's ground operations in Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched almost 200 missiles at Israel on Tuesday evening, causing scattered damage and fires from falling shrapnel, but Israeli authorities said there were no injuries. An Israeli security official said most of the missiles were intercepted, though some managed to land.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about Hezbollah's capabilities after its recent losses
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah has suffered some of the heaviest losses in its history over the past two weeks, chief among them the killing of its longtime leader, Hassan Nasrallah, in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How does Israel’s Iron Dome work?
item.description: Iran launched missiles toward Israel Tuesday, just days after Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed in a strike on Lebanon. The Iranian blitz comes after a nearly yearlong cross-border conflict between Hezbollah, a star proxy group for Tehran, and Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Japan's new premier vows to push strong defense under Japan-US alliance and boost economy
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The newly elected Japanese prime minister pledged Tuesday night to stick to the vital Japan-U.S. alliance amid growing tension in the region while calling for it to be more equitable.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels launch drone boat that hits ship in Red Sea as missile strikes another
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels launched an explosive-loaded drone that crashed into one ship Tuesday in the Red Sea and a missile that exploded against another.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Russian fighter jet flies within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The U.S. military is slamming Russia for an “unsafe” encounter in the skies off the coast of Alaska after footage released Monday shows a Kremlin fighter jet cutting off an Air Force F-16. The incident, which took place Sept.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: IDF meets little resistance from Hezbollah after weeks of hitting terror targets, officials say
item.description: The Israel Defense Force met little resistance from Hezbollah terrorists despite weeks of growing anticipation of an Israeli incursion amid strikes and raids by specials ops units in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels are looking to gain from continuing conflict in the Middle East
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — In the days since Israel intensified its campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon, including the strike that killed the militant group's leader Hassan Nasrallah, Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels have been quick to show they are an important player in the complex conflicts convulsing the M
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's new prime minister vows to curb debt as he faced baptism of fire at heckled parliament
item.description: New French Prime Minister Michel Barnier faced a rough ride at parliament as he laid out his proposed policies in an inaugural speech that highlighted how difficult governing will be for his minority government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tibetan group in India protests against China and human rights situation in homeland
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Dozens of Tibetans living in India protested outside China's embassy on Tuesday against the human rights situation in their homeland, which China annexed in 1951.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel And Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taiwan shuts schools and offices ahead a direct hit from powerful typhoon
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan closed schools and offices and evacuated hundreds from vulnerable areas around the island Tuesday ahead of a strong typhoon expected to hit its densely populated western coast after lashing northern Philippine islands.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah terrorists engaged in sex slavery, rape, mass murder of Syrians
item.description: Startling new information has been released about Hezbollah’s enslavement and rapes of women amid Israel’s decapitation of senior Hezbollah terrorists over the last two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Along Mexico's Pacific coast, flooding from Hurricane John left devastated towns and 17 dead
item.description: COYUCA DE BENITEZ, Mexico (AP) — Along Mexico’s southern Pacific coast, floodwaters receded Monday, leaving behind devastated towns and 17 dead, after John struck the coast once as a hurricane and again as tropical storm last week.
item.dc:creator: FÉLIX MÁRQUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rescuers search for missing people in Nepal following flooding and landslides that killed 224
item.description: Rescuers in Nepal are searching for two dozen people still missing and trying to recover the bodies of those killed in weekend flooding and landslides that left more than 200 dead.
item.dc:creator: Binaj Gurubacharya, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Who is Claudia Sheinbaum, the scientist who will head Mexico as its first female president?
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum takes office Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president in the nation’s more than 200 years of independence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel begins ground offensive against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli ground forces crossed into southern Lebanon early Tuesday, marking a significant escalation of an offensive against Hezbollah militants and opening a new front in a yearlong war against its Iranian-backed adversaries.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, ABBY SEWELL and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indian-controlled Kashmir votes in final phase of polls to elect local government
item.description: Voting has begun in Indian-controlled Kashmir in the final phase of an election to choose a local government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The release of the video comes after a series of Russian incursions into the Alaska Air Defense Identification Zone.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian fighter jet intercepted near Alaska by NORAD forces after ‘unsafe, unprofessional’ interaction
item.description: North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) released a video showing the dangerous moment a Russian Su-35 fighter pilot flew right in front of a NORAD F-16 fighter pilot over the Alaska Air Defense Zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah says it will choose Nasrallah's successor 'at the earliest opportunity'
item.description: Hezbollah’s second-in-command, deputy leader Naim Qassem, says the terrorist group will choose its new leader "at the earliest opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says he will talk to Netanyahu as Israel pummels Sunni terror targets in Beirut
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces reportedly began striking the Lebanese capital of Beirut on Sunday evening after President Biden said he intended to speak with Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in hiding with extra security following Hezbollah leader's death: report
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was placed in a secure location after Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah's killing in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel tells its troops to prepare for a possible ground operation in Lebanon
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel is preparing for a possible ground operation in Lebanon, its army chief said Wednesday as Hezbollah fired dozens of rockets across the border and a missile aimed at Tel Aviv that was the militant group's deepest strike yet.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, TIA GOLDENBERG and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US pushes to secure Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: NewsNation is told the diplomatic discussions are not centered on a possible link of a ceasefire with Hezbollah to one for the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Guterres tells UN meeting that impunity, inequality and other challenges risk engulfing the world
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The head of the United Nations warned gathered leaders Tuesday that impunity, inequality and uncertainty are driving modern civilization toward “a powder keg that risks engulfing the world" — the latest clarion call from Antonio Guterres that the global situation is becoming in
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation
